---
title: "Creating A Parent Class"
---

---
title: "Creating a Parent Class"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/dyh1755684496336"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

You can create a parent class that contains all the default fields associated with it. The parent class is displayed with a lock icon next to it. You can create child classes which contain the fields obtained from the classified documents.

The Ignore Class for Auto-Classification feature enables the parent class to be locked and not have any documents assigned to it.

To create a parent class:

1.  On the Class page, click + Create Class. The Add Class dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
2.  Enter a name for the document class in the Name field. .  Note: The document class name must be unique.
3.  Enter a description for the document class in the Description field.
If you want to create another class, click Create another and repeat steps 1 and 2.

4.  Optional. Click the Options tab, under General settings, enable the Ignore Class for Auto-Classification option to prevent automatic classification of documents for the selected class. A lock icon is displayed next to the classes for which this feature is enabled.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  Click the + icon to create a child class. The Add Child Class dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)   Enter the details, as required.
6.  Click Save. The child class is created and is displayed under the parent class.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Note:
- To modify the name of a class, select the relevant class and click the Edit class ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon.
- To delete a class, select the relevant class and click the Delete class ![](data:image/gif;base64,) icon.
- If you delete a parent class, a confirmation message is displayed. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
- To delete the parent class along with its child class, click Delete All.
- To delete only the parent class and keep the child class, click Delete and Detach. The child class changes to an individual class with fields.
You can also detach a child class from its parent class. To detach a child class from a parent class:

1. Click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Detach class from parent. A confirmation message is displayed. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
You can choose to keep or remove its linked fields. Do either of the following:

2. Click Detach and Remove Fields to detach the parent class and remove the linked fields.
Or,

3. Click Detach and Keep Fields to detach the parent class but retain the linked fields.Note: This action cannot be reversed.
